zh.lifeSummary: 當Alise Cloughe於1545年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可蕾托 瑟洛福時，她的父親Richard Cloughe25歲，母親Hannah Cloughe20歲。她於1570年與Richard Livermore在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1594年在英國英格蘭享年49歲，埋葬在英國艾可蕾托 斯特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Cloughe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a precipitous slope, Middle English clo(u)gh, clou, clew (Old English clōh) ‘ravine, steep-sided valley’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example in Lancashire, Cumbria, or Yorkshire.
English: in the East Midlands, East Anglia, and southeastern England, probably more often a variant of   Clow  .

variants.name: Clow
meanings: English (East Midlands and East Anglia): probably from Middle English cloue, a false singular form of Middle English clouse ‘bar, enclosure, narrow pass’, later ‘mill dam, sluice-gate, floodgate’. See Clowes .
English and Scottish (southern and central Scotland): variant of   Clough   1.
English (Devon): from an earlier form of modern Cornish cleath ‘dyke, earthwork’, as found in the placename Clovelly (Devon) and most likely a habitational name referring to that place.

variants.name: Fairclough
meanings: English (Lancashire): from one or more lost or unidentified places, probably in Lancashire, named with Middle English fair(e) ‘beautiful’ + clo(u)gh ‘narrow valley, ravine’ (see Clough ). Fairclough Farm in Chipping, Lancashire is a candidate for the surname's origin, although early forms of the placename are lacking. A number of the earliest bearers came from Shevington, Lancashire, where the narrow valley of the River Douglas may perhaps have have been known in the medieval period as Fairclough.

nameMeanings.name: Alise
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Alise Cloughe was born in 1545, in Little Thurlow, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Cloughe, was 25 and her mother, Hannah Cloughe, was 20. She married Richard Livermore in 1570, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1594, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Little Chesterford, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alise Cloughe est née en 1545, à Little Thurlow, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Cloughe, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Hannah Cloughe, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Livermore en 1570, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1594, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Little Chesterford, Essex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Alise Clougheが1545年にイギリスイギリススフォルクリットル スウルロウで生まれた時，父Richard Clougheは25歳，母Hannah Clougheは20歳であった。1570年にイギリスでRichard Livermoreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1594年にイギリスイギリスで49歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスリットル チェステルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alise Cloughe(이)가 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 리틀 스를로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Cloughe(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hannah Cloughe(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1570년 영국에서 Richard Livermore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1594년 영국 잉글랜드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 리틀 체스터퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Richard Livermore em 1570, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alise Cloughe родилась в 1545 г. в местности литл турлоу, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Cloughe было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Cloughe, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Livermore в 1570 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1594 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности литл честирфорд, эссекс, Англия.
